### Totals

- **Left turns (roughly half):** ~**1,400** from highways/secondary roads

## Estimating Left Turns: Lisbon to Singapore by Road

### Route Overview
The shortest road route runs approximately **17,000 km**, passing through roughly: Portugal → Spain → France → Northern Italy/Balkans → Turkey → Iran → Pakistan → India → Myanmar → Thailand → Malaysia → Singapore (~13 countries).

### Framework: Turns per Kilometer by Road Type

| Segment | Distance | Road Type | Sig. turns/km | Total turns | Left turns (~50%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Portugal/Spain | 1,500 km | Motorways | ~0.07/km | 105 | 53 |
| France/Alps/Balkans | 1,800 km | Mix motorway/mountain | ~0.12/km | 216 | 108 |
| Turkey | 1,500 km | Highway network | ~0.10/km | 150 | 75 |
| Iran | 1,500 km | Good highways | ~0.09/km | 135 | 68 |
| Pakistan | 1,000 km | Mixed roads | ~0.15/km | 150 | 75 |
| India | 3,000 km | Dense network, cities | ~0.20/km | 600 | 300 |
| Myanmar | 700 km | Secondary roads | ~0.18/km | 126 | 63 |
| Thailand | 1,500 km | Highways + urban | ~0.12/km | 180 | 90 |
| Malaysia/Singapore | 500 km | Urban highways | ~0.15/km | 75 | 38 |

**Total estimated left turns: ~870 directional + ~600 intersection-based urban turns ≈ ~1,500**

### Key Considerations
- The majority of distance is on highways where sharp turns are rare
